title: Cannot build from inside container (Doop), getting: OCI permission denied
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

#### Simplified (updated):

```powershell

# ✅ works
podman run --rm -v /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:cli docker ps
# ❌ doesn't work
podman run --rm -v /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:cli sh -c "echo -e 'FROM scratch' | docker build -t doop -"

# Compare to docker
# ✅ works
docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:cli docker ps
# ✅ works
docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:cli sh -c "echo -e 'FROM scratch' | docker build -t dood -"
```

#### Original:
1. Create a Dockerfile
```
FROM alpine
CMD ["echo", "Hello World"]
```
2. Run to verify that that docker out of podman works:
```bash
podman run -it --privileged -v /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v .:/src docker:cli docker ps
```
3. Run a build command which will fail:
```bash
podman run -it --privileged -v /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v .:/src docker:cli sh -c "cd /src && ls . && docker build -t hello ."
```


### Describe the results you received

The output:

```
Dockerfile
[+] Building 1.7s (1/1) FINISHED                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 docker-container:default
 => ERROR [internal] booting buildkit                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                1.7s 
 => => starting container buildx_buildkit_default                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    1.7s
------
 > [internal] booting buildkit:
------
WARNING: No output specified with docker-container driver. Build result will only remain in the build cache. To push result image into registry use --push or to load image into docker use --load
ERROR: failed to build: Error response from daemon: crun: create `/sys/fs/cgroup/docker`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

The expectation would be that it is something along the lines of this:

🚀  podman build -t hello .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:1074353eec0db2c1d81d5af2671e56e00cf5738486f5762609ea33d606f88612
Copying config sha256:e7b39c54cdeca0d2aae83114bb605753a5f5bc511fe8be7590e38f6d9f915dad
Writing manifest to image destination
STEP 2/2: CMD ["echo", "Hello World"]
COMMIT hello
--> 0a8ca940f2a7
Successfully tagged localhost/hello:latest
0a8ca940f2a7d4da7821630d127312e6f16ada23f5702e69ee0334b232a59426

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running this on Windows. Tried both rootless and privileged.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I can confirm the behavior. It's due to rootless mode.
@jankapte I don't believe it works in privileged either

AFAIK, rootless/full and privileged are different things.

I could reproduce exactly your error message when using rootless podman machine. This is why I had to go back to rootful podman machine and your examples does work (note the different socket mount path):

Example with root*full* podman machine:
```
podman run --rm -v /var/run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:cli sh -c "echo -e 'FROM scratch' | docker build -t doop -"
#0 building with "default" instance using docker-container driver

#1 [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile
#1 transferring dockerfile: 50B done
#1 DONE 0.0s

#2 [internal] load .dockerignore
#2 transferring context: 2B done
#2 DONE 0.0s
WARNING: No output specified with docker-container driver. Build result will only remain in the build cache. To push result image into registry use --push or to load image into docker use --load
```

Unfortunately I have not saved the results for the rootless machine, but it's exactly what you describe above:
> ERROR: failed to build: Error response from daemon: crun: create `/sys/fs/cgroup/docker`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

Yes, it does work for rootful when I run it like this:

```
podman machine init --now rt --rootful
podman context use rt-root
podman run --rm -v /var/run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:cli sh -c "echo -e 'FROM scratch' | docker build -t doop -"
```

Should it still work for rootless? Why does rootless get to do `ps`, but not build?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jankapte I'm also facing this. I would like to run rootless. The problem went away when I disabled buildkit, but I would like to not disable buildkit. Is there a solve for this?
